segments.text: Lesson 11.

translation.text: 第11课。

segments.text: Not Guilty.

translation.text: 无罪。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What was the customs officer looking for?

translation.text: 海关官员在找什么？

segments.text: officers are quite tolerant these days, but they can still stop you when you are going through the green channel and have nothing to declare.

translation.text: 如今海关官员相当宽容，但即使你走绿色通道、没有东西需要申报，他们仍可能拦住你。

segments.text: Even really honest people are often made to feel guilty.

translation.text: 即使真正诚实的人也常常会被弄得觉得自己有罪。

segments.text: The hardened professional smuggler, on the other hand, is never troubled by such feelings, even if he has 500 gold watches hidden in his suitcase.

translation.text: 另一方面，老练的职业走私犯从不会受这种感觉困扰，即使他的手提箱里藏着500块金表。

segments.text: When I returned from abroad recently, a particularly officious young customs officer clearly regarded me as a smuggler.

translation.text: 最近我从国外回来时，一名格外爱管闲事的年轻海关官员显然把我当成了走私犯。

segments.text: Have you anything to declare?

translation.text: “你有什么要申报的吗？”

segments.text: He asked, looking me in the eye.

translation.text: 他直视着我的眼睛问。

segments.text: No, I answered confidently.

translation.text: “没有。”我自信地回答。

segments.text: Would you mind unlocking this suitcase, please?

translation.text: “请把这个箱子打开，好吗？”

segments.text: Not at all, I answered.

translation.text: “当然不介意。”我答道。

segments.text: The officer went through the case with great care.

translation.text: 官员非常仔细地翻查了箱子。

segments.text: All the things I had packed so carefully were soon in a dreadful mess.

translation.text: 我精心收拾好的所有东西很快便被翻得一团糟。

segments.text: I felt sure I would never be able to close the case again.

translation.text: 我确信自己再也无法把箱子合上了。

segments.text: I saw the officer's face light up.

translation.text: 我看见官员脸上露出了喜色。

segments.text: He had spotted a tiny bottle at the bottom of my case and he pounced on it with delight.

translation.text: 他发现箱底有一个小瓶子，欣喜地扑了上去。

segments.text: Perfume, eh?

translation.text: “香水，嗯？”

segments.text: He asked sarcastically.

translation.text: 他讥讽地问。

segments.text: You should have declared that perfume is not exempt from import duty.

translation.text: “你本该申报，香水可不免进口税。”

segments.text: But it isn't perfume, I said.

translation.text: “可这不是香水，”我说。

segments.text: It's hair gel.

translation.text: “这是发胶。”

segments.text: Then I added with a smile.

translation.text: 接着我微笑着补充道：

segments.text: It's a strange mixture I make myself.

translation.text: “这是我自己调制的一种奇怪混合物。”

segments.text: As I expected, he did not believe me.

translation.text: 不出所料，他不相信我。

segments.text: Try it, I said encouragingly.

translation.text: “你闻闻看。”我鼓励他说。

segments.text: The officer unscrewed the cap and put the bottle to his nostrils.

translation.text: 官员拧开瓶盖，把瓶子凑到鼻孔前。

segments.text: He was greeted by an unpleasant smell which convinced him that I was telling the truth.

translation.text: 一股难闻的气味扑鼻而来，使他相信我说的是实话。

segments.text: A few minutes later, I was able to hurry away with precious chalk marks on my baggage.

translation.text: 几分钟后，我便带着行李上那几道可贵的粉笔放行标记匆匆离开了。
